title: How to Fix Bluetooth Device Not Showing on iPhone 17 / 17 Pro Max!
transcript: Bluetooth device not showing up on your iPhone 17, 17 Pro, or Pro Max. It can't find any nearby Bluetooth devices. Here's how to fix it in seconds. First of all, make sure the Bluetooth device that won't pop up is actually in pairing mode. So, follow the instructions for the device. And in most cases, you'll need to press and hold the pairing button until the device is discoverable. Then, it will pop up on your iPhone. If it doesn't pop up, then let's force restart our iPhone, which can fix any software glitches and may as well solve the problem. For this, short press the volume up, then quickly the volume down, and then hold down the side button and keep holding until your phone turns off and the Apple logo appears. If the problem persists after the restart, then your Bluetooth device may be already paired to some other one. And to fix this, what you can do is reset the Bluetooth device. And after you have reset it, it should appear on your iPhone settings automatically. Some of your iPhone's content and privacy restrictions can also cause this issue. So let's open up settings. Scroll down to screen time. Go to content and privacy restrictions. And from here, first make sure Bluetooth sharing is not disabled. Or you can also turn off content and privacy restrictions altogether and see if that helps. Is the device still not appearing? Then what is necessary is trying another Bluetooth device and see if that one is discoverable. If the other device pops up, then you know it's not your iPhone and the device that you're trying to pair. But unfortunately, if your iPhone is unable to find any Bluetooth devices, there's a chance that it's an hardware error and you can find it out yourself if you boot up your iPhone in diagnostics mode. So, if you're willing, then first turn off your iPhone and while it's turned off, hold down both the volume buttons and while holding the buttons, you need to insert the charging cable. Keep holding until the Apple logo appears. Now, let go and you'll be in diagnostics and repair mode. Now, unplug the power cable, press continue, connect your Wi-Fi, and tap on run diagnostics. But if it shows green, then you can get out of the recovery mode by pressing on the three dots, exit diagnostics, exit. title: El iPhone 17 Pro Max en JAPÓN 🇯🇵
transcript: That's why I'm so fed up with wanting to buy the iPhone 17 Pro Max in Japan.  And it's because I've been trying to buy the new iPhone for more than 15 days.  The first time 15 days ago this Japanese girl told me there was no stock and after waiting two weeks I came back again, but this Japanese girl told me there was none either. She started explaining to me that there's a secret trick to getting a new iPhone.  He told me that many people come in the morning to buy iPhones and they line up before the store opens.  So all those people who arrive in the morning are guaranteed an iPhone, and that's why today I got up at 7:48 in the morning and when I went out into the street it was freezing cold and on top of that it was raining, so I practically ran towards the subway and once I was on the subway it turns out that the subway was undergoing maintenance, so many cars were delayed and to top it off I went at the time when all normal people go to work and it was packed, there was no room, people were fighting for space.  Even when I got on, I felt like someone was giving me a scare, to be honest.  But don't worry because I finally got to the Apple Store and realized that even then there were n't that many people. And he tells me, "But the 2 TB one, which costs practically twice as much as the normal one."  I told him, "No, man, dude, I refuse to spend that kind of money on a cell phone."

title: iPhone 17 Pro Review a Week Later: Here's My Experiences So Far
transcript: Hey everyone. So, it's been exactly 1 week since I made this my main phone, the iPhone 17 Pro. And you know, I came directly from the iPhone 15 Pro. I actually skipped the 16 Pro entirely because I was waiting for a jump that felt well really worth it. And after 7 days of using this as my daily driver, I have some thoughts. In this video, we're going to cover everything. We'll talk about this new design and yeah, the switch back to aluminum. We'll look at the performance, which is honestly kind of shocking. The cameras, battery life, and at the end, I'll give you my honest take on whether this is the year to upgrade, especially if you're coming from an older iPhone. So, what's so special about this phone? When it was announced, a lot of the talk was about Apple ditching the titanium from the last couple of years. Was that a downgrade? Well, it's complicated. Let's get into it. Okay, first up, the design. And honestly, when I first saw the leaks of this camera plateau instead of the old bump, I thought it was a bit weird, but having it in my hands for a week, I've completely changed my mind. I actually love it. It makes my old 15 Pro feel, I don't know, a little dated. Let me know in the comments if you feel the same way. But what's more important is how it feels. Because of this unibody aluminum and these more rounded sides, it's just so much more comfortable to hold and use than the last few Pro models. It's really comfy to operate. Now we have to talk about the material. Apple went back to aluminum from titanium. And look, holding it next to a 15 or 16 Pro, you can just feel the difference. Titanium feels a lot denser, but not that heavy. The stainless steel on the 14 Pro and older also felt really premium and strong, though it added some weight. This is lighter. But let's be real about why. This aluminum body combined with a new vapor chamber inside is a gamecher for thermals, and we'll get to that in a minute. But it comes with a trade-off. You've probably heard about scratch gate. A lot of the early reports and videos from people on forums, especially on the cosmic orange and deep blue models, showed the anodized finish scratching and chipping really easily. It seems to happen most around the sharp corners of this camera plateau. Even YouTubers like Jerry Rig everything showed in his test that while the phone is durable, the anodized coating on those sharp edges is just more brittle. That's honestly the main reason I went with this silver color, it tends to hide micro scratches way better, and I think it just looks clean and minimalist. But yeah, if you get a darker color, you'll probably want a case on day one. Just remember, aluminum is a softer metal than titanium. As for the display, it's still that 6.3 in size from the 16 Pro, which yeah, is a bit bigger than my old 6.1 in 15 Pro, but the bezels are so thin, and with the rounded sides, it really doesn't feel that much bigger in the hand. The real upgrade here is the brightness. It can now hit 3,000 nits outdoors, which is up from 2,000 on previous models. In simple terms, you can see the screen perfectly, even in super bright direct sunlight. And they've added a new anti-reflective coating that actually makes a noticeable difference. It's just a gorgeous display. Okay, let's talk performance because this is where things get kind of insane. This phone has the new A19 Pro chip and look, I ran Geekbench 6 on it and you can see the scores right here on screen. 3,822 on single core and 9,848 on multi-core. To put that multi-core number in perspective, that is faster than the M2 chip in the iPad Pro. I'm going to say that again. This phone has the multi-core performance of a desktop class chip. It's just wild. But here's the thing. Peak performance numbers don't tell the whole story. The real magic here is the new vapor chamber cooling system. This is the reason they switched to aluminum. And I felt it from the moment I turned this phone on. You know how iPhones always get warm during that initial setup while everything is downloading and indexing? This one didn't. It stayed completely cool. And over the past week, with all my normal use, from casual browsing to moderate heavy work, I have not felt this phone get hot. The only time it got a little warm was when I was outside shooting in the new 4K 120 fps mode in direct sun. And even then, it was just warm, not uncomfortably hot. This is a massive, massive upgrade for thermals and temperature management. I haven't even gotten around to heavy gaming yet, but with this chip and this cooling system, I have zero doubt it can handle any game you throw at it, from casual stuff to AAA titles. The cameras on the 17 Pro got a huge overhaul. All three lenses on the back, the main, the ultra wide, and the telephoto are now 48 megapixels. The detail you can get is just incredible. But for me, the best change is the zoom. They went with a four bikes telephoto lens this year, not the five bikes from last year's 16 Pro. And I personally love this. 4x is such a great focal length for portraits and just everyday shots. You get these beautiful optical zoom options at.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x, and even a really clean 8x. And the digital zoom can now push all the way to 40x, which is a massive, insane quality jump for me. For video, you can now shoot in 4K at 120 fps, which is amazing for super smooth slow motion. But the most fun and maybe most functional upgrade is actually the front camera. Apple is calling it the center stage camera. It has this new square sensor, which means you can take a landscape selfie or video without ever turning your phone sideways. And for group shots, it uses AI to automatically widen the view to fit everyone in. Honestly, this is one of those features that I fully expect every other phone company to copy next year. It's that good. Of course, none of that performance matters if the battery can't keep up. And I'm happy to report the battery life on the 17 Pro is fantastic. It's a bigger battery than previous models thanks to the new internal layout. And it easily gets me through a full heavy day of use with no problem. After a week, my battery health is of course still at 100% with just three charge cycles. So, this is peak performance and it's really, really good. Quick note on software and Apple intelligence. Yes, this phone is built for it with new neural accelerators in the A19 Pro chip, but let's be honest, for me, it's not a big deal right now. Apple Intelligence is still in its very early days. title: iPhone17 Pro1ヶ月使用レビュー｜16 Proと何が変わった？買い替える必要ある？
transcript: Is it necessary to switch to the iPhone 16 Pro ? A new iPhone is released every year , and just like every year, there are many opinions flying around saying that this year's model has evolved, and that it is necessary to upgrade .  Conclusion: Existing iPhone 16 Pro users can upgrade to the 17.  Well, this year's 17 Pro is a bit on a different level of evolution than previous models.  This time, I'll talk about the improvements I've noticed after using the iPhone 17 Pro for a month, after using the iPhone 16 Pro for a year.  We will now discuss in detail which of these two knights you should buy, focusing on the specs and the changes in the user experience . The iPhone 17 Pro has changed from last year's Tita model to a one-piece aluminum unibody construction.  The wide camera bump makes it immediately obvious that this is the latest iPhone .  The design of iPhone models hasn't changed in recent years, so this year I felt even more excited to have the latest model .  The length and width dimensions have remained almost the same, but the weight has increased by 7g and the actual thickness has increased by about 0.7mm.  When you look at the numbers, it seems like it's really only a little bit thick , but when you hold it in your hand and compare it to the other phone, you can really get a sense of how thin and heavy it is .  The 16 Pro is thinner and lighter. However, the aluminum unibody's new cooling system, the Bay Chamber, provides excellent heat dissipation .  This clearly improves on the weakness of previous iPhones, where performance would decrease due to heat even when taking photos with the camera for long periods of time or playing games .  It doesn't feel like the heat is concentrated and trapped in one place , but rather that it is dissipated throughout the entire unit.   The grip is slightly rounded, making it easy to hold even without a case.  It fits really well in the hand.  Well, last year's model of the Titan has been given a more luxurious feel and a slightly thinner body , so I personally preferred the 6T model in terms of design and texture , but it's definitely more practical.  Well, the design has been improved and it feels like there has been a major evolution in terms of cooling the internals .  Yes, the screen is exactly the same as the Super XTR Display 16Pro .  The only difference is that the outdoor peak speed has increased from 2000 to 3000.  Well , the most important evolution is in the improved behavior at the time of delivery .  One of the biggest complaints about the 16 Pro is that when playing heavy games or recording long videos, the screen would automatically reduce its brightness to prevent overheating .  In contrast, this phenomenon almost never occurs with the 17 Pro .  This is thanks to improvements in the thermal management and cooling system of the entire system, going beyond the evolution of the display itself .   It also seems that the A19 Pro chip has achieved highly efficient and stable operation, so the system no longer needs to request power restrictions on the display .  The appeal of the 17Pro is that it is much brighter even when shooting in the summer sun , and there are fewer shadows when looking at the screen, so you can use it outside for long periods of time without worry .  It was an interesting discovery to see that this year's thermal design optimizations also affect the display experience .  Chip performance: Just looking at the spec sheet, it has simply gone from A18Pro to A19Pro. However, the actual experience is quite different. The iPhone 17 Pro has been proven to sustain approximately twice the performance of the 16 Pro .  This is the result of a radically improved thermal design, but honestly, I could feel a significant difference in gaming performance .  Comparing the same scene, the 16 Pro's refresh rate became unstable over time and the screen felt rough, whereas the 17 Pro's refresh rate was stable and there was no noticeable roughness .  Even when doing things like 3D, which is particularly demanding, or long video recordings, the device doesn't get hot like it did with the 16 Pro, and the screen doesn't go dark, so it almost never wakes up .  When it comes to battle scenes in 3D games, the refresh rate makes a clear difference in the gaming experience.   There's no doubt that both offer high performance, but the appeal of the 17Pro is that it can maintain that high performance for a longer period of time .  At first, Apple's expression "sustainable high performance" sounded a bit exaggerated, but after actually using it and playing games with it, I understood why it made sense .  Well, the camera is a bit plain, but it has been updated.  The iPhone 16 Pro only had a 12MP telephoto lens, but the 17 Pro has three rear cameras, all 48MP, which improves color, sharpness, image quality, and consistency .  The telephoto camera has been completely redesigned and now features 48 larger sensors. Details remain even when used at night or in dark places .  The sensor size is 56% larger than the 16Pro.  It can capture more light, allowing for telephoto shooting in dark places.  The image quality is significantly improved .  However, when it comes to normal shooting, like 1x , 2x, etc., I don't feel like there's any dramatic improvement or change in image quality.  When it comes to taking photos with the 16Pro, the quality is already incredibly beautiful and mature.  Oh, and this year the front camera has been updated.  18P center frame front camera. When taking a selfie with people next to you, you previously had to turn the iPhone sideways , but thanks to the square sensor frame, the angle of view is wider even when held vertically, making it easier to take pictures .  Well, of course this doesn't just apply to taking selfies, but also to phone calls, LINE video calls, and more .  By placing it vertically like this, you can talk to the other person while looking at their face.  Of course, the image quality of the in-camera is also good.  This is a subtle but significant improvement over the 16 Pro.  Oh , and long video recordings.  As explained above regarding cooling performance, the A19 Pro's high sustained performance significantly reduces the risk of heat-related stalls or performance degradation when shooting high-bitrate video formats such as 4K 60FPS pro-video In addition, the 17Pro supports Pro Lezzro and Apple 2 as professional features .  It has expanded the scope of expression for creators .  I want to easily make cinematic vlogs for YouTube.  Oh, so you've provided a format that allows people like that to have a new visual experience .  I want to record my child's growth beautifully.  I want to leave beautiful memories.  So perhaps the days of thinking, "Should I buy a camera?" are coming to an end .  Batteries have also improved significantly.  The iPhone 16 around 3580m.  The iPhone 17 Pro is said to be around 4250m.  The numbers have changed by about 700m, and you can see that the battery life has increased , but you can also feel the difference .  After a full day of use, it feels like it has about 20% to 30% more power than the 16 Pro .  Apple's official video playback time is up to 22 hours for the 16 Pro and up to 33 hours for the 17 Pro .  11 hours is a pretty big amount, isn't it? Especially when using it for video recording or gaming , in my case, even on days when I did a lot of video recording , or even a lot of gaming, the battery still had plenty of battery left until the evening, and with the 16 Pro there were many times when I needed to use a mobile battery during the process , but now I hardly ever need to.  And charging performance has also improved.  The iPhone 16 Pro is said to support 30W, while the 17 Pro supports 36W.  Well, that's the time it takes to charge from 0 to 50%.  The iPhone 16 Pro took about 30 minutes to complete, while the 17 Pro took around 20 minutes .  Yes, charging is reliable and generates little heat .  Oh, and the fact that the device doesn't heat up as easily even during high-speed charging is a subtle but welcome improvement.  And the price.  The iPhone 17 Pro now starts at 179,800 yen.  Well, at first glance it seems expensive .  It's extremely expensive.  However, considering that the 16TPro sells for around 1.23 million yen used, the additional cost of switching from here would be around 50,000 yen.   For a fee of 50,000 yen, you can double the heat problem relief .  Battery life is an extra 9 hours. Also, the minimum storage capacity has increased from 128GB to 256GB .  Considering these things, I honestly feel like the cost performance is high same applies to new players who were planning to purchase these two knights. Well, especially for those who use high-end apps, games, or video production, the work efficiency and stability will be worth more than 50,000 yen .  On the other hand, if you're a light user who mainly uses social media and makes calls, the 16T Pro is of course fine.  However, for those who plan to use it lightly, I would recommend this year's Muji model, as it is extremely well-made.  I guess the option of buying the 16 Pro now is almost gone.  So to summarise what's been said so far, it looks like this: Well, the iPhone 17 Pro started by eliminating the issues of heat generation and reduced start-up time, and then improved the durability of the A19 Pro. Well, all cameras are now 48MP, which unifies colors and improves dark areas, and the battery life has increased and the charging speed has become faster.  Well, it's actually an update that costs 50,000 yen, and the cost performance is quite satisfactory .  What I've noticed while carrying the iPhone 17 Pro every day for the past month is that it offers high-performance processing that can be used continuously.  Problems such as the screen going dark due to heat and unstable operation have been resolved. And the battery life has improved.  The accumulation of these small changes has greatly reduced the stress in my life .  However, that doesn't mean that the device itself doesn't get hot; it heats up relatively quickly when playing games or shooting videos .  It does get hot, but it's not like the load is concentrated in one spot around the chip; rather, the entire body is heated slightly, allowing the heat to escape, and it's clear when comparing the two models that the body has been changed to one that can maintain high performance Rather than significantly improving performance in one area, the evolution is in the direction of maintaining stable high performance.   That's why I feel that people who watch videos, play games, and take photos on a daily basis should be the ones to benefit from this .  So, I would have shared a video saying that there wasn't much improvement this year and that there's no need to buy a new one, but I really love the 17 Pro.  As for the color , well, this time I bought the orange one , and at first I thought it was really bright and cool, but, well, to be honest.  Well, if I were to buy one now, I think I'd go for a whitish silver or navy color Well, I'm sure everyone who bought the iPhone 17 Pro is watching this video too.  Well, I'm sure there are some people out there who are considering buying it.  Well , please feel free to write your opinions in the comments section .  Yes, if you found this video even a little helpful, please click the like button and subscribe to our channel. Well, I've also posted videos about the iPhone 17 standard model and why you don't need to buy the iPhone Air, so please take a look if you're interested. See you again in the next video. Thank you for watching until the end

title: iPhone 17 Pro atau 17 Pro Max? Jangan Sampai Nyesel Karena Salah Pilih!
transcript: The iPhone 17 series will reportedly be available at the end of November or early November.  I'm sure some of you still can't decide whether to take the Pro or the Pro Max.  Even though it seems like this is a trivial matter .  However, this is not as trivial as you might imagine.  We will spend around 5 million Rupiah on a smartphone that we will use every day.  It will be taken everywhere, taken traveling, put in a pocket, even accompany us to sleep, for example.  Don't choose the pro and then regret it later because you didn't choose the Pro Max or vice versa, you choose the Pro Max and it's too big in your hand.  Even though the plan is to use it for the next 3, 4, or even 5 years, for example.  I also plan to upgrade to the latest iPhone this year and I'm also considering whether to stay with the Pro Max this year or switch to the 17 Pro.  That's why I made this video, hopefully it can give you an idea of ​​which iPhone is best for your needs.  Is it 17 Pro or Pro Max.  And I would be very grateful if you also support my channel so that it can grow even further.  Previously, several iPhone Pro series had different features, especially in the camera section. Currently, the only difference between the two is the screen size, weight of the iPhone, and battery life.  So, whatever you choose, there are no reduced features.  Personally, this year I'm more interested in the iPhone 17 Pro than the Pro Max.  And I have a few important reasons why I'm most likely switching to the Pro variant this year.  Even though previously I always used the Pro Max variant.  First of all, the size. Previously, the Pro variant was very small and could be said to be too small.   The iPhone 11 Pro is 5.8 inches and the Pro Max is 6.5 inches.  the size of an iPhone now.  Then it became 6.1 and 6.7 inches on the iPhone 12 Pro to the 15 Pro Series.  And on the iPhone 16 Pro and 17 Pro Series the size has increased by 2 inches, to 6.3 and 6.9 inches.  In my opinion, the 6.3 inch size fits perfectly in the hand.  Not too small and not too big either.  That's why I chose the Pro Max variant before because I took the screen size into consideration.  Now the pro screen size is really ideal in the hand. I've also seen it directly at the official reseller and checked how it feels in the hand.  Is it still comfortable to text or reply to chats with one hand and also how heavy is it?  I have a travel tips YouTube channel where I record all the footage with old iPhones and an iPhone 14 Pro Max which weighs 1/4 kilo plus a thick case.  I have to hold it like this when I want to talk in front of the camera and it makes my hands shake and my little finger becomes concave like this.  I went on a road trip to Flores to the top of Limutu without bringing an action cam.  Finally, when I was riding a motorbike on that trip, I recorded with one hand like this and the other hand holding the motorbike steering wheel. I had to grip it really tightly like this because the wind was also quite strong and my iPhone could fall at any time because it was blown away or hit.  Luckily, it didn't fall.  From there, I thought to myself, hopefully, in the next iPhone release, Apple will give us something really important that we actually want to get the Pro, but because it's not in the Pro series, people end up getting the Pro Max, namely battery life.  Besides the 13 Pro and 16 Pro, the previous Pro variants of the iPhone were definitely wasteful and heated up quickly because of their small bodies, overpowered chipsets, small battery capacities, and bodies that were less able to dissipate heat.  And because before I buy something, I always watch reviews from lots of tag channels to make sure that I won't make the wrong choice. Almost all reviewers say the battery life of the iPhone 17 Pro and Pro Max is the best.  compared to previous iPhones.  In addition to Apple giving a larger battery capacity to the series and Pro Max this year, the addition of the Viper Chamber also makes the battery performance of the iPhone 17 Pro and Pro Max more durable and the iPhone does not heat up quickly, including the Pro variant too.  Even on The Tech Chap's YouTube channel, the iPhone 17 Pro's battery life is slightly longer than the 16 Pro Max's.  Do you have an idea?  An iPhone that fits perfectly in your hand, has a super powerful chipset, and battery life that can last as long as last year's 16 Pro Max. That's my strong reason for switching from the usual Pro Max because of the screen and battery considerations to the Pro variant.  Apart from that, I rarely edit videos from my iPhone. In fact, I moved the footage to the Mac and then edited there.  But that doesn't mean the 17 Pro is anything special .  Of course, the Pro Max series has more advantages in terms of screen real estate. The 17 Pro Max wins a lot, especially with the brightness setting up to 3000 nits, which is best for scrolling through social media, watching streaming, or playing games on the 17 Pro Max's large screen.  For those of you who like editing photos or videos to post on social media, the large screen of the 17 Pro Max is supported by the greatness of the A19 Pro chipset and 12 GB of RAM, making your video editing experience even smoother. In short, the large screen is really helpful for those of you who do anything on your iPhone, from entertainment, games, editing content to work matters, all of which can be fulfilled.  Yes, it's actually really possible to edit photos or videos using the 17 Pro, but the screen isn't big enough, so our eyes get tired quickly if you edit photos or videos from the 17 Pro's screen for a long time.  The next advantage is definitely the larger battery capacity.  And now it's the iPhone with the longest battery life compared to previously released iPhones.  Many say their iPhone can last all day until tomorrow with high intensity use .  Meanwhile, for normal use, it can last up to 2 days.  I'm sure Promx fans will still choose the Pro Max whatever the reason.  Some people buy it because of the bigger screen and longer-lasting battery, and there are also those who buy the Pro Max variant so people know they can buy a more expensive iPhone. Because it looks bigger physically.  Meanwhile, regarding memory, who knows if the iPhone 17 Pro we bought is 256 GB or 2 TB.  But if you look at the physical size, the difference is clearly visible.  If you really buy Prox to be considered better, then there is no cure for that.  But I'm sure I'm not the only one who chose the Pro Max variant because the previous Pro variants were battery-guzzling and heated up quickly.  We also think that we are buying this cellphone for the long term.  If you just bought it and it's really wasteful and heats up quickly, how will the battery's health be after 1 year and beyond?  Thankfully, Apple has improved battery life this year , especially for the standard iPhone 17 Pro .  In conclusion, all the features on the 17 Pro Max are also on the 17 Pro.  So, whatever you choose is the same.  The difference between the Pro and Pro Max now is only in the screen size and battery life.  I still recommend visiting the nearest reseller shop to check for yourself which one feels most comfortable in your hand.  And what do you guys often use it for? If you're going to spend a lot of screen time watching streaming, playing games, scrolling through social media, editing photos or videos, and don't mind the iPhone's heavier weight and larger body, the iPhone 17 Pro Max is more suitable for you.  But if you want a smaller iPhone size, it's easy to carry in your pocket, easier to reply to chats with one hand, and it's also light in weight for vlogging activities. With a battery life that is now on par with last year's Pro Max series, the iPhone 17 Pro could be the right choice.  Hopefully this information is useful. Thank you for watching and see you in the next video.

title: iPhone 17 Pro : mon avis 1 mois plus tard !
transcript: The iPhone 17 Pro is undoubtedly one of the models that brings great new features while also having choices that are divisive.  The biggest change, obviously, is its design, and I think it's a direction that makes sense for the Pro range.  It features an all-new unibody aluminum design with a wide tray for the rear cameras, replacing the titanium frame of the iPhone 16 Pro, and now comes in three colors: deep blue, silver, and cosmic orange.  Visually, the iPhone 17 Pro looks much more utilitarian, more like a tool than a beautiful smartphone.  It's no longer a luxurious design like before, but it's not ugly either.  I would even say that it is quite successful, just very different from what the pro range had accustomed us to.  And when we understood that it no longer adopts the same philosophy as the old pro models with their stainless steels and more recently their titanium which were almost considered technological jewelry objects.  It is understood that Apple's show, with its all-aluminum look, wants to break away from this precious look in favor of something more functional.  This also leaves room for the iPhone, which maintains this approach.  This choice of aluminium has consequences, of course.  He already scores more easily.  We've also seen quite a few photos showing how quickly the blue model can wear out.  The slightest scratch reveals the raw silver color underneath.  Apple may have even avoided releasing a black model this year, precisely to avoid those micro-scratches that would have been too visible.  For my part, I've been using the iPhone 17 Pro for several weeks now and honestly, I had a hard time choosing the color this year. To tell you the truth, during the presentation, I hated this cosmic orange and I was hoping for a black version, so I went for the blue version that you saw in my unboxing video by the way and after a few days in hand, I didn't appreciate this blue.  I don't know, he's handsome, but he wasn't right for me in the end. So I sent it back and hesitated for a long time between the silver and orange colors.  Ultimately, I went with the opposite choice to what I initially thought.  And I took the orange one. By using my link in the description, you will benefit from a unique offer to test Nor VPN with 4 months of free subscription on 2-year subscriptions. Thank you to them for supporting the channel this year. Beyond the color, it must be emphasized that Apple has truly made a turning point with this more robust design.  The iPhone 17 Pro is a tiny bit thicker and heavier than the 16 Pro, bringing a larger battery inside.  At the rear, a new glass plate is noticeably broken, along with the Unibody design in aluminum.  In fact, it's essential to allow the search to pass without a MaxSafe film.  This new design with a glass back and aluminum frame is not just aesthetic. It also helps this iPhone to dissipate heat better.  The famous vapor chamber introduced this year is actually sealed within the aluminum housing so as to efficiently dissipate heat from the processor throughout the entire phone.  The heat is therefore better distributed.  There are fewer localized hotspots than we have seen until now.  And when I edit photos on Lightroom, the iPhone heats up less.  However, the phenomenon of automatic brightness reduction when the iPhone heats up is drastically reduced.  So yes, this new vapor chamber made possible in part thanks to the aluminum chassis really changes things on a daily basis, having gone from the iPhone to the iPhone 17 Pro and vice versa, you really feel the difference.  This design change also marks a new distribution of roles within the Apple product range.  The 17 Pro has become the practical and high-performing iPhone to the point of losing some of the luxury aspect that was expected on these versions. Meanwhile, Apple launched a brand new model this year, the iPhone, which takes over the torch of premium design. The iPhone has a much more attractive design.  He is impressed by its thinness and lightness, with a thickness of only 5.6 mm and a weight of 165 g.  It is quite simply the thinnest and lightest iPhone ever created by Apple.  I think he couldn't release two smartphones with the same features, otherwise the iPhone would never have had its place.  As a result, the Pro is geared towards being a professional tool and the iPhone is geared towards being a design object.  If I had to make an analogy, the iPhone would be more like the chino where the iPhone 17 Pro was more like the cargo.  Do you see the idea?  In terms of power, the new 19 Pro continues to widen the gap in almost every area: CPU, GPU, Neurol Engine.  Everything is faster, smoother.  And thanks to the new cooling system I mentioned earlier, the 17 Provient maintains this level of performance over time, without overheating or slowing down while gaming, editing 4K, or using NIA for the few times it's available .  It teaches while remaining surprisingly cool in the hand, unlike the others which became completely boiling hot.  This also inevitably affects the battery life and I have to be honest with you, it didn't impress me.  I was expecting better, and there might be a reason for that: in Europe, we have a smaller battery than in the United States.  This is due to the fact that in Europe, we have a SIM card tray on the iPhone 17 Pro, which the iPhone R does not have, and which the iPhone 17 Pro which comes from the United States, for example, will also not have; we will have an ISIM card inside and therefore a larger battery.  So when I push the iPhone to its limits shooting professional video in high resolution, it inevitably drains the battery.  However, for more typical use, it easily lasts all day and better than an iPhone; that's where you can feel that the A19 Pro chip, the optimization, and the slightly larger battery do the job well.  I think that with this slightly larger battery, we would really have the gap between the models.  That part is a little frustrating, it's true. The most interesting part of this new iPhone is, of course, the camera.  For me, that's the number one reason to get this iPhone 17 Pro. The balance between photo and video is just ideal and I find that currently few other smartphones offer such reliability in these two areas while being so versatile.  Moreover, Apple proudly announces having h Pro lenses in our pocket thanks to this 17 Pro.  So, we still have three physical sensors, but this year they are all 48 megapixels, covering a huge range of different focal lengths through cropping and combination.  The telephoto lens, for example, goes from a 5x optical zoom on the 16 Pro to a 4x zoom on the 17 Pro, which may seem less good, but in reality this 4x zoom now uses the entire range of the 48-megapixel sensor, which then allows the 8x digital zoom to be used without major loss of quality, obtaining a very decent 12-megapixel image.  The photos produced by these 48 megapixels are sublime, much more detailed.  I mostly shoot in Proro format, then I apply my own Lightroom presets and it completely changes the mood of the images. And then the real surprise is that the main sensor is also slightly improved, being a bit larger than on the 17 iPhones, with results that are still excellent. We have clearly reached a point where the iPhone is increasingly replacing a professional camera in my daily life. But the iPhone 17 Pro has become so practical, especially with its three 48-megapixel lenses, AR, top-notch stabilization, LC you want, all in your pocket, it's crazy, it's a camera and a phone and a smartphone for all other apps.  Its main sensor is larger and will be of better quality than the iPhone and iPhone 17. It is sharp, detailed with accurate colors.  As for the zoom, it is frankly of very good quality.  Even though I rarely use it, the 5x zoom was never useful to me.  So , the 8x zoom doesn't really make sense for my use. And then this 48-megapixel ultra-wide-angle and wide-angle mix is ​​a joy to use with a real depth of field via the sensor, while also being able to add an extra layer with portrait mode .  In video, it is just as excellent, very well balanced.  I've used it quite a bit for a big project coming up, a beautiful renovation mixing tech. Just like the entire 17 range and the iPhone, this 17 Prose has an 18-megapixel front sensor in a square format allowing you to switch from portrait mode to landscape mode without having to change the orientation of your iPhone.  I do n't take many selfies, but for those who do regularly, this is a really welcome update, and the photo and video quality is much better.  I hope that next year we will have even higher quality with possibly a larger sensor to balance the front and rear quality a little bit, although well, we won't use it that much, but it's true that it's lacking a little bit.  I would especially like it on a Pro version, that could justify it.  Another major strength of the iPhone 17 Pro is that the screen brightness now reaches 3000 nits in direct sunlight.  On a daily basis, you can feel it; you can see the screen much better, even in the car or when taking photos. It's a real plus, like an anti-reflective coating that was a little more optimized.  This makes me think that we now have Ceramic Shield 2 to protect the screen, which is said to be even more scratch-resistant. Personally, I don't use a screen protector on my iPhone.  Audo, rarely, that's also why we buy a smartphone for its overall aesthetics.  On the front, and in view of the recent setbacks in recent years, I still prefer to put some on.  But this year on the 17 Pro, I made a bet that the Ceramic Shield would hold up, we'll see in the long term.  Of course, I will keep you updated on my Instagram account, so feel free to follow me there.  In any case, after several weeks and carrying it around a bit like a real professional tool, there are no problems so far. Looking at the range a little more closely, I don't think the 17 Pro is the most relevant choice this year.   As you've seen, it's an excellent iPhone with high performance, whether in photos, video, or everyday use, it's amazing.  But paradoxically, the one that seems the most balanced is the standard iPhone 17.   In my opinion, this is the one with the fewest compromises, the best value for money, and the one that will suit the majority of people.  It has everything you would expect from a good iPhone and even more compared to the iPhone.  It is made of green and aluminum, like the Pro models.  It is slim, aesthetically pleasing, lightweight, comfortable to hold and above all it incorporates almost all the new features of the range.  It has a 6.3-inch screen like the iPhone 17 Pro, the green-free promotional technology that eliminates the distinction between Pro and non-Pro models in terms of screen size.  Everything is smooth, it's a real treat.  We have the customizable action button on the side and even the camera button.  The A19 chip, which for typical use can run everything needed, even for gaming or photo editing.  He's doing well.  The only difference is that it will get hotter.  Excellent battery life, sometimes even better than the Pro model.  The new 18-megapixel Center State front camera, like other iPhones.  A 48-megapixel main sensor plus a 48-megapixel ultra-compank , which the iPhone does not have.  It has Dolby Vision 4K at 60 fps and even a cinematic mode like the iPhone 17 Pro. And of course, the USB-C port with fast charging.  In short, the iPhone 17 has everything a pro phone has, except the price.  The only real differences concern very professional, even expert functions such as the vapor chamber for cooling or the professional photography.  But honestly, for €969 these compromises are totally acceptable, especially compared to an iPhone which costs almost as much as a pro model, but omits quite a few features.  This is an iPhone that had to make many sacrifices to achieve its feat.  An iPhone that clearly doesn't suit everyone, and that's precisely what makes the iPhone 17 so interesting.  It is versatile, comprehensive, and much more logical for the majority of users. In summary, the iPhone Air is a gamble on design and emotion.  The iPhone 17 is the most versatile with the best price-to-use balance, and the iPhone 17 Pro is the professional tool tailored for those who want the very best in photos, videos, and hardware.  The 17 Pro is an excellent iPhone but it is aimed at advanced users who want a performance tool above all else and not an object of admiration.  If you are coming from an iPhone 16 Pro, the iPhone 17 Pro is clearly not essential. The changes are there, but it's not revolutionary by any means.  Since it's a 15 Pro, why not?  It's something to think about. But especially from a 14 Pro or earlier, that's when you'll really see a technological leap.  Looking back, I'm quite happy with Apple's work this year on the different product lines. I think it makes things much clearer.  I speak as if I were at the investors' table.  Joking aside, Apple is laying the groundwork for a new cycle. The iPhone Air ushers in a new era of design.  The Pro range truly lives up to its name, and the iPhone 17 becomes the right choice by default.  For my part, I'm still a little undecided. On one side, there is the elegance, the finesse, the innovation of the iPhone, with an added emphasis on almost artistic emotion, and on the other side, the power, versatility and rigor of this iPhone 17 Pro which is useful to me as a professional user .  Two radically different visions of the same product.  If this iPhone intrigues you, feel free to check out my dedicated video on the channel.  I show you why this is a unique iPhone, a real bet on desire, perhaps even a bet on the future of Apple.  Who knows?  That was Quentin.  Have a great day everyone.

transcript: Is this the iPhone that's most worth it from this year's Apple 17th series ?  At least that's what everyone says.  And after reviewing the Pro Max and Air models, it's time to check if this statement is true.  Shop at ardes.bg with the promo code The Clashers for a 3% discount.  Smartphones, laptops, computer components, peripherals and gaming. By entering it every time, you support the channel.  Thank you. Today, my friends, the iPhone 17 arrived for review. Short, precise and clear, without endings like Air Pro or Max.  This basic version, as you can see, has much more compact dimensions, a neat and minimalist look, and most importantly, no plateau on the back.  On the other hand, we have an improved display, a new chip, a 48- megapixel ultra-wide sensor, which we also got, as well as the new square selfie camera.  In the end, we can say basic, basic, but how basic is it?  This one, dear friends, received more upgrades from the pro series compared to last year's equivalents. However, before I share with you all the improvements of this new generation, let's do a quick unboxing. Unboxing, as always, goes by in one breath with a product like this, and the phone's screen shines in front of the lens for us to enjoy before it gets smeared with fingerprints.  We look at some other details on the chassis, then I return to the box, in which we receive a Type-C charging cable, a pin, and booklets.  We end the boxing with a general plan of the set and continue with the review.  After the passions and conflicting opinions about the pro version of this year's 17s subsided a bit, I decided to take a look at the slightly more classic model, which is absolutely identical to the 16s in appearance, but receives a lot of hardware improvements.  Let's start with the design first.  The matte back is glass and the frame is made of recycled aluminum.  On the left side of the phone we will find the SIM card slot, which can naturally be combined with a SIM.  And above the volume controls is an action button, which is programmable.  From there, you can set various shortcut functions, and I have anchored Visual Intelligence.  On the right side at the top we have a power button, and below it for the second year we get a camera control button, from which you can activate and navigate the camera menu.  The C port on the bottom, however, unlike the pro version, is 2.0, which means much slower transfer speeds.  But to be honest, I don't even remember the last time I transferred data from my phone to my computer via a cable.  I always use Airdrop, and yet maybe that's why we don't have Pro Race Roll support in the normal 17, as for it, if you remember, you had to connect an external SSD.  This is how we take advantage of the higher speeds of Type C 3.2 in pro models.   The camera module on the back, I admit, is much more compact and nicer than the one Apple launched this year.  But if you put the 17 on its back, the wobble is significant. We have five color options, including lavender, green, and blue, in addition to, of course, the standard black and white. Otherwise, the phone weighs 177 g and feels very pleasant in the palm of your hand.  The inner frame around the screen is even thinner than last year's 16-inch model and is equal in thickness on all sides.  Visually, these are perhaps the only differences.  And when we touched on the topic of the display this year, hallelujah.  For the first time, Apple is introducing Pro technology into its base models.  So, we finally move from the prehistoric 60hz refresh rate to the modern 1 to 120hz.   It was about time.  This also unlocks the ability to use the alwaysone feature, where the display will drop to 1xz.  Another cool extra is that here you can also now disable the pulse width modulation, which I told you what it does in more detail in my Pro Max review. Separately, the screen also features an anti-reflective coating, which reduces reflections by 33%.  A Raamic Shield 2 protective coating is also available for three times better scratch protection than the previous version.  The Super red XDR OLED screen is now larger at 6.3 inches compared to 6.1 inches on the 16th and accordingly receives a new resolution of 262 by 1206 pixels with a density of 460 PPI. Brightness also marks a step forward, with peak reaching 3000 nits like the pro models, and typical being 1000. The base model was definitely in dire need of this screen upgrade, and so Apple actually patched the huge gaping hole between it and their pro models .  However, let's see what hardware improvements they have made under the glass-aluminum shell of this device.   The three-nanometer A19 chip has six CPU and five GPU cores, and we also have a 16-core engine designed for acceleration in AI tasks and computing.  [music] In terms of RAM, the phone is equipped with 8 GB, similar to last year, but we see a double increase compared to the base capacity, which is now 256 GB, and the maximum is 512. In terms of performance, of course, it is slightly inferior to the Bro.  However, in my opinion, this is something that can only be noticed in benchmark graphs.  In everyday phone usage, I didn't notice any difference compared to the Pro Max model.  Games run smoothly, and there's nothing remarkable about heating up, even though the phone doesn't take advantage of the Vaper chamber built into the Proto.  Yes, the back will warm up slightly, with this being felt more in the upper half of the chassis, but again nothing out of the ordinary. Reputable sources mention that the battery of the 17-card is 3692 matches.  Apple does not share such data. [music] If this is true, it means that the model with the SIM card slot has a slight improvement over last year's version.  Video playback reaches up to 30 hours according to Apple, but this is a fairly relative statistic, considering that it would be affected by the brightness and what resolution you watch the videos at.  In terms of charging, you can charge the battery to 50% in just 20 minutes, but if you use the brand's new 40-watt Power Delivery Charge, I would be interested in how long it will take from 0 to 100, but since I don't have it, and it's not widely available in Bulgaria, I can't tell you the charging time.  Separately, we also have 25-watt wireless QI charging and 4.5-watt charging, but via cable.  You can use it to get some juice into your headphones. The phone also has IP68 water resistance and will withstand diving up to 1 meter, with air reaching it for no more than 30 minutes.  We turn the page from hardware to software to tell you that the 17 comes with iOS 26 and the liquid glass look that debuted this year. I like the interface and system customization options and Quiid definitely gives the phone a refreshed look. AI and Telllin, however, are still stuck in the abyss of AI technologies that Apple has gotten itself into, but at least you can take advantage of features like Visual Intelligence to recognize locations, objects, or text, as well as generate images from Playground. Another good thing I can mention is that we have seven years of software updates.  In terms of connectivity, the iPhone 17 supports Wi-Fi 7 as well as Bluetooth 6.0 thanks to the N1 Network chip, and in terms of performance, everything is top notch. However, after all these technical details, let's see the cameras in action.  On the back we now have two 48 megapixel sensors that bin their photos to 24 megapixels and 12 when you use the digital zoom. The main camera features sensor-shift optical stabilization and takes excellent shots with balanced colors and good detail, without excessive processing from the phone.  For daytime shots, you can choose between 24 and 48 megapixel photos, but while I was digging through the menu to enable this option, I noticed that they had inserted a detail.  Night photos, whether with or without flash, are always 12 megapixels. However, in low light, the main camera does quite well, with a slight difference in quality noticeable with the dual-lens camera .  In bright light, however, the 2x zoom is visible much better, and if you activate portrait mode, it will automatically switch to it with this zoom.  [music] The Ultra with the new sensor is guided by the same rules for megapixels and this camera gives us a much more voluminous and detailed shot.  In terms of colors, it again seems to me that the two cameras are almost identical. In low light, night mode photos on the ultrawide were also satisfactory, but sometimes it takes a few seconds to capture the frame, which feels like an eternity. The phone also uses the ultra-wide sensor for macro shots.  Although when you're on the main camera and zoom in on the macro lens, it doesn't say anywhere that it switches to the wider camera, even though it does.  This gives the impression that you are taking macro shots with the main camera, but that 's not the case.  In terms of video, both cameras give us a maximum of 4K at 60 frames per second, and the difference in stabilization between them is hardly noticeable, even though the main one has optical and the wide one has digital.  There is also a slow motion option at 120 and 240 frames, but it is in Full HD resolution.  It's time to test Apple's infamous new square front camera, which you can see me through right now.  Its sensor is 24 megapixels, but it shoots at 18 because it can't do a mode.  Openate is when we use the full size of the sensor. The big extra here is that you can switch between horizontal and vertical shooting modes without having to rotate the phone, with just the press of a button.  You can also record video with the rear and front cameras simultaneously with a moving bubble on the screen.  However, I remind you again that it creates one file, not two.  Otherwise, the video recording quality of all cameras on the device reaches 4K at 60 frames per second and you can judge for yourself how it looks and sounds.  I also leave some muffin selfies in vertical and horizontal mode for comparison.  In my final thoughts, I would say that the 17 is truly a significantly improved phone over the 16, and if you're wondering whether to get it, I think it's worth it.  The 120hz promotional display is really a different beer compared to last year's 60hz.  We also got a new ultrawide camera, a new selfie camera, a brand new chip, and a brand new starting base capacity of 256 GB. Overall, there hasn't been such a big upgrade from Apple to a new phone compared to last year's in a long time.  God grant that every year every subsequent base model will be worth this. Naturally, by tradition, we start with the positives. First of all, I definitely put the pro screen. title: Apple iPhone 17 Pro – Die richtige Wahl in diesem Jahr? (Review)
transcript: Hello and welcome to my YouTube channel Xbertech.  My name is Patrice and today we're taking a look at the iPhone 17 Pro, which I've been using for just under a month. As always, we'll go through all the categories and finally discuss the price and whether purchasing Apple's latest compact flagship is worthwhile for you .  And before we begin, I have one small request for you.  Leave me a comment, a like, and most importantly, a subscription when you have a chance.  That would help me a lot.  And now, on we go after the intro.  See you soon. [Music] Normally we would now turn to the contents of the iPhone 17 Pro's box, but let's be honest, Apple was the pioneer and the first manufacturer in 2020 to eliminate the last item from the box . Therefore, I will deliberately keep this part short.  Besides the iPhone 17 Pro, the box also contains a SIMTOR tool, a USB-C to USB cable, and some paperwork.  And that's all .  Now we turn our attention to the design, the feel, and the workmanship.  And here, the iPhone 17 Pro polarizes opinion like no iPhone before it has in a long time. And that starts with the materials used.  Because unlike in the past two years, we now have not a titanium frame or a full glass back, but an aluminum unibody, which is protected here by some ceramic slab.  But on the front we also have Seramic Glass 2 and an anti-reflective coating.  And that was it. Otherwise, as mentioned, aluminium is the new standard here.  This is primarily intended to aid heat dissipation and ensure better heat removal .  Nevertheless, this design is thoroughly polarizing .  Some love it, others hate it.  I am a huge fan of this design.  I like this incredibly much.  It really has a cool character.  It's a statement.  Overall, I also find the aluminum picture really, really pretty.   It feels incredibly good to the touch. Especially now, because the frame is a bit thicker.  With a thickness of 8.8 mm and a weight of 206 g, the device fits incredibly well in the hands.  So it is a very, very, very well-balanced device.  As you can see, if I keep it this way, it's a really, really nice balance, and in my opinion they did a really good job with that.   Of course, I can also understand that many people say, we had a titanium frame, we had a glass back, that looked very classy, ​​very businesslike, and yes, this now looks more like a work tool, a real pro tool with pro features.  I really like it, and it's strange to say it's the coolest and most beautiful iPhone I've had in years .  Therefore, it gets the absolute best rating from me here, even though I definitely know that many see it differently, but yes, tastes are known to differ.  Next, we will quickly cover three categories: biometrics, speakers, and haptic feedback.
